1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. session.assistant: Good! There's a TUI documentation folder. Let me check the runs-tab-architecture.md file which looks directly relevant:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: "Runs Tab Architecture" 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with TUI Runs tab" 5→ - "adding columns to RunDataTable" 6→ - "understanding branch resolution in runs" 7→ - "modifying workflow run display in TUI" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "using run.branch directly for display without checking PR head_branch first" 10→ warning: "After merge+deletion, run.branch becomes master/main. Use PR head_branch as primary source, falling back to run.branch only if not master/main." 11→ - action: "adding a new column to RunDataTable without updating RunRowData" 12→ warning: "RunDataTable columns are populated from RunRowData fields. Add the field to RunRowData first, then add the column in _setup_columns." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Runs Tab Architecture 16→ 17→The Runs tab displays GitHub Actions workflow runs in the TUI, alongside Plans, Learn, and Objectives views. 18→ 19→## RunRowData 20→ 21→Frozen dataclass representing one workflow run row. 22→ 23→**Source**: `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` 24→ 25→| Field | Type | Description | 26→| ------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | 27→| `run_id` | `str` | GitHub Actions workflow run ID | 28→| `run_url` | `str \| None` | URL to the GitHub Actions run page | 29→| `status` | `str` | Raw status: "queued", "in_progress", "completed" | 30→| `conclusion` | `str \| None` | Raw conclusion: "success", "failure", "cancelled", None |
... [78 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: View-Mode-Aware Help Screen 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the TUI help screen" 5→ - "adding view-specific actions to the TUI" 6→ - "understanding ViewMode-conditional rendering" 7→--- 8→ 9→# View-Mode-Aware Help Screen 10→ 11→The TUI `HelpScreen` accepts a `view_mode: ViewMode` parameter and conditionally renders help content based on the active view (plans vs objectives). 12→ 13→## Implementation Pattern 14→ 15→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py --> 16→ 17→The `HelpScreen.__init__()` stores `view_mode` as an instance variable. A helper method `_is_objectives_view()` wraps the check. The `compose()` method uses this to branch rendering: 18→ 19→```python 20→def compose(self) -> ComposeResult: 21→ is_objectives = self._is_objectives_view() 22→ # ... shared sections ... 23→ if is_objectives: 24→ yield Label("Enter View objective details", ...) 25→ else: 26→ yield Label("Enter View plan details", ...) 27→``` 28→ 29→## Conditional Sections 30→
... [26 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI Data Contract 3→read_when: 4→ - "building an alternate frontend consuming plan data" 5→ - "adding fields to PlanRowData or PlanDataProvider" 6→ - "understanding the display-vs-raw field duality" 7→ - "serializing plan data to JSON for external consumers" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "formatting display strings during table render" 10→ warning: "Display strings are pre-formatted at fetch time. Add new *_display fields to PlanRowData and format in RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data(), not in the widget layer." 11→ - action: "adding a field to PlanRowData without updating make_plan_row" 12→ warning: "The fake's make_plan_row() helper must stay in sync. Add the new field with a sensible default there too, or all TUI tests will break." 13→ - action: "putting PlanDataProvider ABC in src/erk/tui/" 14→ warning: "The ABC lives in erk-shared so provider implementations are co-located in the shared package. External consumers import from erk-shared alongside other shared gateways." 15→ - action: "constructing PlanFilters without copying all fields from existing filters" 16→ warning: "All fields must be explicitly copied in _load_data() PlanFilters construction. Missing fields (like creator) cause silent filtering failures." 17→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 18→audit_result: edited 19→--- 20→ 21→# TUI Data Contract 22→ 23→The TUI data layer is designed around a single frozen dataclass (`PlanRowData`) that carries both display-ready strings and raw identifiers. This document explains the design decisions behind the data contract, the cross-package split, and the pitfalls of JSON serialization — things that can't be learned by reading any single source file. 24→ 25→## Why Display and Raw Fields Coexist 26→ 27→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/data/types.py, PlanRowData --> 28→ 29→`PlanRowData` carries two kinds of fields for the same data. For example, `pr_number` (raw `int | None`) and `pr_display` (pre-formatted `str` like `"#123 👀"`). This is deliberate: 30→
... [215 lines omitted] ...
143→| `run_conclusion` | `str` | Yes | Workflow run conclusion: `"success"`, `"failure"`, `"cancelled"`, etc. | 225→Adding a field to `PlanRowData` touches at minimum five places. Missing any one causes test failures or silent data loss:

1→"""Data types for TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import dataclasses 6→from dataclasses import dataclass 7→from datetime import datetime 8→from typing import Any 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import IssueFilterState 11→ 12→ 13→@dataclass(frozen=True) 14→class PlanRowData: 15→ """Row data for displaying a plan in the TUI table. 16→ 17→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 18→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 19→ 20→ **Display vs Raw convention:** Many data points have both a raw field (for 21→ logic/predicates) and a display field (for table rendering). Raw fields are 22→ often nullable (None when absent), while display fields are always strings 23→ (dash "-" or empty when absent). Use raw fields in availability predicates 24→ (``ctx.row.pr_number is not None``), display fields for rendering. 25→ 26→ Attributes: 27→ 28→ plan_id: GitHub issue number (e.g., 123). Never None. 29→ plan_url: Full URL to the GitHub issue. None when unavailable. 30→ full_title: Complete untruncated plan title. Empty string possible.

58→ run_conclusion: Run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). 167→ conclusion: Raw run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). None if in progress. 219→ warnings: tuple[str, ...] = ()

1→"""View mode types for TUI dashboard.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from dataclasses import dataclass 6→from enum import Enum, auto 7→ 8→ 9→class ViewMode(Enum): 10→ """Available view modes for the dashboard.""" 11→ 12→ PLANS = auto() 13→ LEARN = auto() 14→ OBJECTIVES = auto() 15→ RUNS = auto() 16→ 17→ 18→@dataclass(frozen=True) 19→class ViewConfig: 20→ """Configuration for a specific view mode. 21→ 22→ Attributes: 23→ mode: The view mode this config describes 24→ display_name: Human-readable name for the view 25→ labels: GitHub labels to use when fetching data 26→ key_hint: Key binding hint (e.g., "1", "2", "3") 27→ exclude_labels: Labels to exclude from results (client-side filtering 28→ applied before expensive enrichment). Empty tuple means no exclusion. 29→ """ 30→

1→"""Run table widget for TUI dashboard Runs tab.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, cast 6→ 7→from textual.events import Click 8→from textual.message import Message 9→from textual.widgets import DataTable 10→ 11→from erk.tui.data.types import RunRowData 12→ 13→if TYPE_CHECKING: 14→ from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp 15→ 16→ 17→class RunDataTable(DataTable): 18→ """DataTable subclass for displaying workflow runs. 19→ 20→ Manages column configuration and row population from RunRowData. 21→ Uses row selection mode (not cell selection) for simpler navigation. 22→ """ 23→ 24→ class RunClicked(Message): 25→ """Posted when user clicks run-id column on a row.""" 26→ 27→ def __init__(self, row_index: int) -> None: 28→ super().__init__() 29→ self.row_index = row_index 30→

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report:
## Summary
I've successfully located all the requested components in the codebase. Here's what I found:
### 1. **RunRowData Dataclass** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/data/types.py` (lines 156-202)
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class RunRowData: """Row data for displaying a workflow run in the TUI Runs tab.
Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. Immutable to ensure table state consistency. """
run_id: str run_url: str | None status: str conclusion: str | None status_display: str workflow_name: str pr_number: int | None pr_url: str | None pr_display: str pr_title: str | None pr_state: str | None title_display: str branch_display: str submitted_display: str created_at: datetime | None checks_display: str run_id_display: str branch: str ```
### 2. **_get_selected_run_row Method** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/actions/navigation.py` (lines 381-390)
```python def _get_selected_run_row(self: ErkDashApp) -> RunRowData | None: """Get currently selected run row data.
Only returns data when on the Runs tab. """ if self._view_mode != ViewMode.RUNS: return None if self._run_table is None: return None return self._run_table.get_selected_row_data() ```
This method: - Checks if the current view mode is RUNS - Returns None if not on the Runs tab - Delegates to `_run_table.get_selected_row_data()` method - Used by action handlers like `action_open_pr()` and `action_open_run()` to get run data
### 3. **ViewMode Enum - RUNS is Already Defined** **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/views/types.py` (lines 9-15)
```python class ViewMode(Enum): """Available view modes for the dashboard."""
PLANS = auto() LEARN = auto() OBJECTIVES = auto() RUNS = auto() ```
**Yes, RUNS is already defined** in the ViewMode enum and is the fourth view mode with key binding "4". There's also a corresponding `RUNS_VIEW` configuration at lines 62-68:
```python RUNS_VIEW = ViewConfig( mode=ViewMode.RUNS, display_name="Runs", labels=(), key_hint="4", exclude_labels=(), ) ```
### Supporting Method The `RunDataTable.get_selected_row_data()` method in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/widgets/run_table.py` (lines 136-145) follows the same pattern as the plan table:
```python def get_selected_row_data(self) -> RunRowData | None: """Get the RunRowData for the currently selected row.
Returns: RunRowData for selected row, or None if no selection """ cursor_row = self.cursor_row if cursor_row is None or cursor_row < 0 or cursor_row >= len(self._rows): return None return self._rows[cursor_row] ```
All three components are properly integrated into the TUI application for handling workflow runs display and interaction.
